arabic_text:
عقل ثالث
ا
ل
س
دیکھ کر آگے بڑھنے والے
لام کے آخری فرد کی دعوت
دعوت پر آگے بڑھنے والے
english_text:
Third Intellect
Alef
Lam
Seen
Those who advance by seeing
Da'wat of the final individual of Lam
Those who advance on Da'wat
alt_text: Two engineering compasses opening at different angles, with two ellipses below containing the Arabic letters 'ل' (Lam) on the left and 'س' (Seen) on the right. Red arrows label the diagram: the left arrow from 'ا' (Alef) → 'عقل ثالث' (Third Intellect) → 'دیکھ کر آگے بڑھنے والے' (those who advance by seeing); the right arrow from 'لام کے آخری فرد کی دعوت' (Da'wat of the final individual of Lam) → 'دعوت پر آگے بڑھنے والے' (those who advance on Da'wat). The compass-and-circle metaphor depicts the geometry of intellectual propagation.
notes: Composite teaching diagram about how Da'wat propagates through successive intellects. The compass imagery is a metaphor for spiritual drawing-out. Letters Alef-Lam-Seen reference a Quranic-letter scheme commonly used in Ismaili esoteric pedagogy.
